leadText: Flamethrower redirects here. For the TFG starter set, see Flamethrower (TFG). Flamethrower (Japanese: かえんほうしゃ Flamethrower) is a damage-dealing Fire-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TM35 from Generation III to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, and in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM37 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR02 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM125 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM062 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I to V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I to V [ edit | edit source ] Flamethrower does damage and has a 10% chance of burning the target. Flamethrower can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Sunny Day was used in the previous turn. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Flamethrower's power was reduced from 95 to 90. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 10 Agile Strong Power 80 60 100 Accuracy 100% 100% 100% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Chance 20% 20% 40% Turns 3 2 4 ? ? Inflicts the target with burn Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 90 108 Cooldown 8s Move wind-up time 20 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect Chance 10% — — May burn the target.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad A powerful Fire -type attack. Has a one-in-ten chance of leaving the target with a damaging burn. Stad2 A powerful Fire -type attack. Has a one-in-ten chance of leaving the target with a burn. G S C An attack that may inflict a burn. R S E Colo XD A powerful fire attack that may inflict a burn. (move) Looses a stream of fire that may burn the foe. R S E (TM) Looses a stream of fire that may burn the target. Colo XD (TM) FR LG The foe is scorched with intense flames. The foe may suffer a burn. D P Pt HG SS PBR The foe is scorched with an intense blast of fire. The target may also be left with a burn. B W B2 W2 The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire. It may also leave the target with a burn. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire. This may also leave the target with a burn. ZA The user attacks with a stream of fierce flames. This may also leave targets with a burn.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance. It may also cause a burn. It thaws and frees frozen Pokémon. MD T D MD S * Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance. It may also afflict the targeted enemy with a Burn status condition. It thaws out frozen Pokémon. MD S * Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance. It may also afflict the target with a Burn status condition. It thaws out frozen Pokémon. B S L はなれたポケモンに ダメージをあたえる しかも いっていのかくりつで やけどじょうたいにかえる こおっていたポケモンは こおりがとける Conq. The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire. It may also leave the target with a burn. MD GtI It damages even a faraway Pokémon. It could also cause the Burn status condition. With the Burn status, the Pokémon keeps taking damage and its Attack also goes down slightly. It damages even faraway Pokémon. It could also cause the Burn status condition. With the Burn status, they keep taking damage, and their Attack also goes down slightly. * Super Smash Bros. for Nintendo 3DS/Wii U Breathe fire. Change the angle with (Joystick). * S MD It damages a Pokémon, even one far away. It could also leave the enemy with a burn. Monopoly: Pokémon Exclusive Kanto Edition Burn up a deed and return ownership to the bank! (Use this power at any time. If the removed property contains improvements, all Poké Marts and Pokémon Centers are sold back to the bank at half the listed price. Payment from the bank is made to the player whose property was removed.)
